source: Maester — MT.1xxx Conditional Access Test Suite
description: Conditional Access policy checks from the Maester MT.1xxx community test suite. These tests validate that your tenant has the required CA policies to meet Microsoft's recommended security baseline. Tests are based on Microsoft documentation and community best practices.

policies.name: Device Compliance CA Policy
policies.fullName: MT.1001 — At least one Conditional Access policy is configured with device compliance
policies.description: At least one Conditional Access policy should require devices to be compliant with the tenant's security configuration to access cloud applications.

policies.name: MFA Required for Admin Roles
policies.fullName: MT.1006 — At least one Conditional Access policy is configured to require MFA for users with administrator roles
policies.description: At least one Conditional Access policy should require multi-factor authentication for users assigned to administrator roles to protect privileged access.

policies.name: MFA Required for All Users
policies.fullName: MT.1007 — At least one Conditional Access policy is configured to require MFA for all users
policies.description: At least one Conditional Access policy should require multi-factor authentication for all users to protect all cloud application access.

policies.name: MFA Required for Azure Management
policies.fullName: MT.1008 — At least one Conditional Access policy is configured to require MFA for Azure management
policies.description: At least one Conditional Access policy should require MFA for access to Azure management interfaces (Azure Portal, Azure CLI, Azure PowerShell) to protect subscription and resource management.

policies.name: Block Legacy Authentication (Other Clients)
policies.fullName: MT.1009 — At least one Conditional Access policy is configured to block other legacy authentication
policies.description: At least one Conditional Access policy should block legacy authentication clients (Other Clients) that do not support modern authentication and bypass MFA.

policies.name: Block Legacy Authentication (Exchange ActiveSync)
policies.fullName: MT.1010 — At least one Conditional Access policy is configured to block legacy authentication for Exchange ActiveSync
policies.description: At least one Conditional Access policy should block Exchange ActiveSync connections which use legacy authentication protocols.

policies.name: Secure Security Info Registration from Trusted Location
policies.fullName: MT.1011 — At least one Conditional Access policy is configured to secure security info registration only from a trusted location
policies.description: Security info (MFA method) registration should be secured via Conditional Access, requiring users to register from a trusted location to prevent registration hijacking attacks.

policies.name: MFA for Risky Sign-Ins
policies.fullName: MT.1012 — At least one Conditional Access policy is configured to require MFA for risky sign-ins
policies.description: At least one Conditional Access policy should require MFA for sign-ins with a medium or high sign-in risk level as detected by Entra ID Identity Protection.

policies.name: Password Change for High User Risk
policies.fullName: MT.1013 — At least one Conditional Access policy is configured to require new password when user risk is high
policies.description: At least one Conditional Access policy should require a password change for users with a high user risk level to prevent compromised accounts from being used.

policies.name: Compliant or Hybrid Device for Admins
policies.fullName: MT.1014 — At least one Conditional Access policy is configured to require compliant or Entra hybrid joined devices for admins
policies.description: Users with administrator roles should be required to use a managed device (compliant or Entra hybrid joined) to access cloud applications.

policies.name: Block Unknown or Unsupported Device Platforms
policies.fullName: MT.1015 — At least one Conditional Access policy is configured to block access for unknown or unsupported device platforms
policies.description: A Conditional Access policy should block access from device platforms that are not explicitly known or managed (e.g. unknown or unsupported platforms) to reduce the attack surface.

policies.name: MFA for Guest Access
policies.fullName: MT.1016 — At least one Conditional Access policy is configured to require MFA for guest access
policies.description: At least one Conditional Access policy should require MFA for all guest and external users accessing cloud applications to protect against guest account compromises.

policies.name: Non-Persistent Browser Session for Non-Corporate Devices
policies.fullName: MT.1017 — At least one Conditional Access policy is configured to enforce non-persistent browser session for non-corporate devices
policies.description: A Conditional Access policy should enforce non-persistent browser sessions for users on non-corporate or unmanaged devices to minimise data leakage risk from shared devices.

policies.name: Sign-In Frequency for Non-Corporate Devices
policies.fullName: MT.1018 — At least one Conditional Access policy is configured to enforce sign-in frequency for non-corporate devices
policies.description: A Conditional Access policy should enforce a sign-in frequency limit for users on non-corporate or unmanaged devices to reduce the risk of session token theft.

policies.name: Application Enforced Restrictions
policies.fullName: MT.1019 — At least one Conditional Access policy is configured to enable application enforced restrictions
policies.description: A Conditional Access policy should enable application enforced restrictions (SharePoint Online / Exchange Online limited access) for unmanaged devices to restrict access to web-only with no download capability.

policies.name: Device Code Authentication Flow Policy
policies.fullName: MT.1052 — At least one Conditional Access policy is targeting the Device Code authentication flow
policies.description: At least one Conditional Access policy should target and restrict the Device Code authentication flow, which can be abused in phishing attacks to authenticate on behalf of users.

policies.name: CA Policy Covering All Cloud Apps
policies.fullName: MT.1003 — At least one Conditional Access policy is configured with All cloud apps
policies.description: At least one Conditional Access policy should be scoped to All cloud apps to ensure full application coverage. Targeted app policies alone leave ungoverned applications exposed.

policies.name: MFA for Guests (External Collaboration)
policies.fullName: MT.1016b — External collaboration requires MFA from guest's home tenant or locally
policies.description: Guest and external users should be required to authenticate with MFA, either satisfied by their home tenant or enforced by a local Conditional Access policy.
